question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send an AddressBookQuery with limit=0 to trigger an unbounded stream of all address book entries (Long.MAX_VALUE items via take()), causing the gRPC server to exhaust memory/threads and deliver stale or partial node topology data to smart contract clients relying on the address book for routing?
